topics_to_register.title: NFTs and the Creator Economy
topics_to_register.description: Learning path exploring how NFTs and blockchain enable direct creator-fan relationships, from Kevin Kelly's 1000 True Fans to Web3 market realities

topics_to_register.title: 1000 True Fans Theory
topics_to_register.description: Kevin Kelly's 2008 theory that creators need only 1,000 true fans paying ~$100/year to make a living, rather than millions of casual followers

topics_to_register.title: Creator Middleman Problem
topics_to_register.description: Centralized social platforms became new intermediaries between creators and fans, inserting ads and algorithmic recommendations while capturing most revenue

topics_to_register.title: NFT Definition
topics_to_register.description: Non-Fungible Token - a blockchain-based record representing unique digital media with verifiable history, provenance, and programmable capabilities

topics_to_register.title: Rent-Seeking Removal
topics_to_register.description: Blockchain-based ownership eliminates rent-seeking intermediaries by giving users full control of purchased NFTs, limiting platform fees through competition

topics_to_register.title: Fine-Grained Price Tiering
topics_to_register.description: NFTs enable creators to capture more consumer surplus through granular pricing from $100K+ items for superfans to $1 items for casual supporters

topics_to_register.title: Users as Owners
topics_to_register.description: Crypto networks turn users into owners through tokens, creating genuine P2P marketing and reducing customer acquisition costs to near zero

topics_to_register.title: Subscribe to Mint
topics_to_register.description: Mirror.xyz product requiring email subscription to mint NFTs, bootstrapping direct creator-collector communication channels for long-term engagement

topics_to_register.title: Genesis Drop
topics_to_register.description: NFT collectible featuring project logo/mascot at launch, adding credibility and catalyzing community formation around project genesis

topics_to_register.title: Collectible Series
topics_to_register.description: Recurring NFT drops providing consistent incentives for new follower subscriptions and brand asset collection completion by existing holders

topics_to_register.title: Writing NFTs
topics_to_register.description: Mirror's approach to tokenizing written content, allowing bootstrapped audiences to collect posts with creators capturing 100% of revenue

topics_to_register.title: Web Evolution
topics_to_register.description: Three phases: Web1 (static sites), Web2 (social/mobile/cloud), Web3 (crypto/AI/decentralization) representing shift from read to read-write to read-write-own

topics_to_register.title: Platform Ownership via Tokens
topics_to_register.description: Web3 enables users to own platforms through tokens, shifting power from centralized corporations to distributed communities

topics_to_register.title: Creator DAO
topics_to_register.description: Decentralized Autonomous Organizations for creators using smart contracts for incorporation, funding, governance, and immediate token liquidity

topics_to_register.title: Curator Economy
topics_to_register.description: Future where curators act like angel investors, building portfolios of NFTs/tokens and proving discovery of creators through transparent on-chain records

topics_to_register.title: Skeuomorphism vs Crypto-Native
topics_to_register.description: Tension between familiar designs mimicking physical objects (YouTube NFTs, Tweet NFTs) versus designs leveraging blockchain's unique properties

topics_to_register.title: NFT Royalty Challenge
topics_to_register.description: 2023 marketplace wars collapsed automatic royalties - OpenSea made royalties optional, Blur enforced 0.5% minimum, revealing gap between code and market reality

topics_to_register.title: NFT Hype Cycle
topics_to_register.description: NFTs followed Gartner Hype Cycle reaching Peak of Inflated Expectations in 2021 ($29B art market), then 93% crash by 2024, with ongoing evolution to practical use cases
